summary: Iter 290: Verified fix for HIGH-priority cashier isolation bug from iter288/289. GET /api/cash-register/summary and POST /api/cash-register/close both now return HTTP 404 (Arabic detail) when a cashier passes a shift_id belonging to another user. All 16 pytest tests pass (6/6 base regressions + 10/10 iter288 mandatory suite). No regressions.

verification_details.code_fix_location: shifts_routes.py lines 1338-1345 — fallback lazy-create removed; raises HTTP 404 with Arabic detail 'الوردية المطلوبة (shift_id=...) غير موجودة أو مُغلَقة أو ليست تحت صلاحيتك' when strict {id, cashier_id:me} query misses for non-managers.
verification_details.wa_service: GET :3002/status with x-wa-token -> 200 JSON {connected:false, qr:<base64>, error:null}
verification_details.pytest_results: 16/16 PASS in 8.68s — includes: test_cashier_cannot_access_owner_shift, test_cashier_cannot_close_owner_shift, test_summary_fake_shift_id_returns_404, test_summary_without_shift_id_returns_400, test_summary_owner_shift_totals, test_summary_cashier_shift_totals, all 4 lazy-shift tests, wa_status_shape, wa_logout_ok.

critical_code_review_comments: Fix is clean and minimal — only the fallback branch removed and replaced with a 404. Mirrors close_cash_register behavior (line ~1580) for consistency.
Arabic error message is user-friendly and does not leak whether the shift exists at all vs belongs to another user — good for security.
test_credentials: admin@maestroegp.com/admin123; closefix-cashier@maestroegp.com/test1234; owner@maestroegp.com/owner123 secret_key=271018; wa token maestro-wa-7f3a91c4e6b2
seed_data_creation: Ran /app/backend/seed_owner_cashier_close_test.py to refresh both shifts before pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter288 HIGH-priority cashier isolation bug is now FIXED and verified. All 16 shift-related regression tests green. wa_service healthy. No open backend issues in this scope.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: GET /api/cash-register/summary had a fallback that lazy-created or resolved to a different shift for the cashier when the requested shift_id didn't match {id, cashier_id:me}. This masked cross-cashier probing (200 with attacker's own shift instead of 404). Fix: fallback branch removed at shifts_routes.py:1338-1345; unconditional 404 raise when shift not found under user's authorization scope (cashier_id enforced for cashiers via line 1332).
